medicinal_uses: Treatment of cough, cold, and bronchitis
Expectorant — loosens phlegm from the respiratory tract
Anti-asthmatic properties
Used for bleeding disorders and wound healing
Anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial
Adathoda.precautions: Avoid during pregnancy. May cause nausea in large doses.

medicinal_uses: Banana flower helps manage diabetes and anemia
Rich in potassium — helps regulate blood pressure
Used for treating diarrhea and dysentery
Stem juice used for kidney stones and urinary disorders
Leaves used as wound dressings in traditional medicine
Banana.precautions: Excessive consumption may cause hyperkalemia in patients with kidney disease.

medicinal_uses: Used in traditional African medicine for skin diseases
Anti-inflammatory properties
Treatment of eye infections
Used as an antidote for snake bites in folk medicine
Antimicrobial activity against common pathogens
Bush Clock Vine.precautions: Limited clinical studies available. Use under guidance.

medicinal_uses: Flower extract used as antipyretic (fever reducer)
Bark used for treating rheumatism and joint pain
Diuretic properties — helps in urinary disorders
Used as a brain tonic and for treating headaches
Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant
Champaka.precautions: Essential oil should be diluted before topical use. Avoid oral intake of oil.

medicinal_uses: Powerful digestive stimulant — improves appetite
Used in treatment of piles and hemorrhoids
Anti-rheumatic and anti-arthritic properties
Treats skin diseases like leucoderma
Hepatoprotective — supports liver function
Chitrak.precautions: Highly potent. Can cause skin irritation. Must be used under expert guidance. Avoid during pregnancy.

medicinal_uses: Leaf paste used for wound healing and skin itches
Anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial
Used in treating respiratory infections
Leaf decoction used for malaria and fever
Mosquito repellent properties
Common Lanthana.precautions: Toxic if ingested — especially berries and leaves. Can cause hepatotoxicity in livestock. External use only recommended.

medicinal_uses: Latex used for treating skin diseases and leprosy
Root bark for fever, cough, and asthma
Flower extract used for digestive disorders
Anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties
Used in Ayurveda for treating elephantiasis
Crown Flower.precautions: Latex is highly irritating to eyes and skin. Toxic if ingested in large quantities. Use under strict medical supervision.

medicinal_uses: Antispasmodic — used in asthma treatment (smoked leaves)
Analgesic for joint pain and rheumatism (external paste)
Used in Ayurveda for hair fall treatment
Sedative properties for insomnia (low doses)
Anti-inflammatory for swelling and boils
Datura.precautions: EXTREMELY TOXIC. Can cause hallucinations, respiratory failure, and death. Never self-medicate. Use only under expert Ayurvedic supervision with proper processing.

medicinal_uses: Root used as purgative and for dropsy
Leaf juice used for wound healing
Anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial
Seeds used in traditional cosmetics
Used for treating abscesses and boils
Four O' Clock Flower.precautions: Seeds and root may be toxic in large doses. Avoid during pregnancy.

medicinal_uses: Promotes hair growth and prevents premature graying
Flower tea lowers blood pressure
Rich in antioxidants — helps manage cholesterol
Used for menstrual regulation and uterine disorders
Cooling agent for body heat and urinary infections
Hibiscus.precautions: May interact with blood pressure medications. Avoid during pregnancy as it may affect estrogen levels.

medicinal_uses: Powerful anti-viral and anti-bacterial properties
Used for treating common cold and flu
Anti-inflammatory — used for sore throat and tonsillitis
Detoxification and blood purification
Used in treating hepatitis and urinary tract infections
Honey Suckle.precautions: Generally safe. Some species' berries may be toxic. Avoid excessive use during pregnancy.

medicinal_uses: Demulcent — soothes inflamed mucous membranes
Used for treating piles, diarrhea, and dysentery
Diuretic — treats urinary disorders
Root decoction used for fever and chest infections
Leaf paste applied for boils and ulcers
Indian Mallow.precautions: Generally considered safe. Consult a practitioner for internal use.

medicinal_uses: Latex used for wound healing and as styptic (stops bleeding)
Leaf decoction used for malaria and fever
Anti-inflammatory paste for skin infections
Bark used for treating gum diseases
Antimicrobial and antifungal properties
Jatropha.precautions: Seeds are highly toxic. Latex can be irritating. Never ingest seeds. Use external preparations only under guidance.

medicinal_uses: Leaf paste used for wound healing and cuts
Treatment of diarrhea and dysentery
Used for toothache and dental ailments
Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant
Root decoction for post-partum recovery
Malabar Melastome.precautions: Generally safe for external use. Internal use should be under practitioner guidance.

medicinal_uses: Anti-inflammatory — used for wound healing and skin repair
Treatment of eye disorders (rich in lutein)
Antiseptic properties for cuts and burns
Used for ear infections and earache
Antifungal and insecticidal properties
Marigold.precautions: May cause contact dermatitis in sensitive individuals. Avoid during pregnancy.

medicinal_uses: Flowers used for bleeding disorders and menorrhagia
Seed oil used for skin diseases and rheumatism
Anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties
Used as astringent for diarrhea and dysentery
Bark used for gastritis and bronchitis
Nagapoovu.precautions: Generally safe in prescribed doses. Consult an Ayurvedic practitioner.

medicinal_uses: Source of vincristine and vinblastine — used in cancer chemotherapy
Leaf juice used for managing diabetes
Used for treating high blood pressure
Antimicrobial and antioxidant
Traditional use for menstrual regulation and malaria
Nityakalyani.precautions: Contains potent alkaloids. NEVER self-medicate. Raw plant consumption can be toxic. Used in modern medicine only in processed pharmaceutical form.

medicinal_uses: Used for treating eye diseases and conjunctivitis
Root bark used as a vermifuge (anti-parasitic)
Flower extract for skin diseases
Analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties
Latex used for treating toothache
Pinwheel.precautions: Latex may irritate skin. Root preparations should be used under guidance.

medicinal_uses: Rose water used as cooling agent for eyes and skin
Rose petal jam (Gulkand) aids digestion and reduces body heat
Rich in Vitamin C (rose hips) — boosts immunity
Mild laxative and blood purifier
Used for treating sore throat and mouth ulcers
Rose.precautions: Generally very safe. Rose oil should be diluted before topical application.

medicinal_uses: Powerful brain tonic — enhances memory and cognition
Used for treating anxiety and depression
Anti-diabetic properties
Root used for treating leucorrhoea and urinary disorders
Rich in antioxidants — anti-aging properties
Shankupushpam.precautions: Avoid during pregnancy. May interact with anticoagulant medications.

medicinal_uses: Bulb paste used for treating swelling and joint pain
Anti-inflammatory properties
Used in folk medicine for treating rheumatism
Antimicrobial activity
Emetic properties (induces vomiting to clear toxins)
Spider Lily.precautions: Bulb is toxic if ingested in large quantities. External use recommended. Keep away from children.

medicinal_uses: Seeds are rich in Vitamin E — antioxidant and skin health
Sunflower oil used for lowering cholesterol
Leaf tea used as astringent and diuretic
Used for treating bronchial and throat infections
Anti-inflammatory properties
Sunflower.precautions: Generally safe. Seed allergy possible in rare cases. High calorie — moderate consumption advised.

medicinal_uses: Flower used for treating dysentery, diarrhea, and leucorrhoea
Root used as sedative and pain reliever
Anti-inflammatory and wound healing
Used for treating hypertension
Bark decoction for treating anemia and general debility
Thechi.precautions: Generally safe. Consult practitioner for internal use.

medicinal_uses: Leaf juice used for treating nasal congestion and sinusitis
Flower extract for cough and cold
Antimicrobial and insecticidal properties
Used for treating headaches — leaf paste on temples
Anti-inflammatory and wound healing
Thumba.precautions: Generally safe for external use. Internal use should be moderate and under guidance.

medicinal_uses: Root used for treating piles and fistula
Leaf paste for wound healing and skin diseases
Used for managing diabetes — reduces blood sugar
Anti-venom properties for snake bites (folk use)
Used for treating diarrhea, dysentery, and insomnia
Touch Me Not.precautions: Mimosine may be toxic in large doses. Avoid during pregnancy. Use in prescribed doses.

medicinal_uses: Excellent wound healer — leaf juice applied on cuts
Hemostatic — stops bleeding effectively
Anti-diabetic properties
Hepatoprotective — supports liver health
Antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory
Tridax procumbens.precautions: Generally safe for topical use. Internal use should be under guidance.

medicinal_uses: Root used as mild laxative and purgative
Diuretic properties — supports kidney function
Used for treating rheumatism and joint pain
Expectorant for cough and respiratory ailments
Root poultice for swelling and inflammation
Wild potato vine.precautions: Root may cause gastrointestinal upset in large doses. Not recommended during pregnancy.

medicinal_uses: Anti-inflammatory — leaf paste for joint pain
Used for treating infections and wounds
Hepatoprotective properties
Used for treating cough and respiratory issues
Antimicrobial and antifungal
Yellow Daisy.precautions: Invasive species in some regions. Generally safe for medicinal use in prescribed quantities.
